# Scan GitLab for secrets

Vault Radar allows you to connect to any top-level groups on GitLab Cloud and GitLab Self-Managed

There are two ways to set up scanning for GitLab -

- Import a single GitLab top-level group as a data source.

- Import many GitLab top-level groups together using a data source host.

To import a single GitLab top-level group as a data source, click **Settings**, then click **Data Sources**.
![Import single GitLab top-level group as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source.png)

To import many GitLab top-level groups together as a data source host, click **Settings**, then click **Data Source Hosts**.
![Import many GitLab top-level groups as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source-host.png)

Additionally, for either option, you can choose to scan with [**HCP Vault Radar Scan**](#scan-with-hcp-vault-radar-scan) or
with the [**Vault Radar Agent Scan**](#scan-with-vault-radar-agent-scan).

# Connect to GitLab

For either the data source or data source host option, scanning can be set up using **HCP Vault Radar Scan** or using **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

## Prerequisites

- [Personal Access Token (with the right set of scopes)](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/profile/personal_access_tokens/#create-a-personal-access-token)

![GitLab personal access token scopes](/img/docs/vault-radar/gitlab/gitlab-pat-scopes.png)

## Scan with HCP Vault Radar Scan

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select either **GitLab Cloud** or **GitLab Self-Managed**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, **Enter your GitLab domain name** or **Enter your GitLab group path** text field may appear.

![GitLab Self-Managed scanned by HCP Vault Radar Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/gitlab/gitlab-self-managed-hcp-data-source.png)

1. If you previously selected **GitLab Self-Managed**, enter the domain URL in the **Enter your GitLab domain name** text field.
    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `gitlab.acme.com`

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources**, enter the top-level group path in the **Enter your GitLab group path** text field.

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your GitLab personal access token** text field.

1. Click **Next**.

## Scan with Vault Radar Agent Scan

1. Make sure that the agent pool has been set up, refer to the [agent pool documentation](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) for more information.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select either **GitLab Cloud** or **GitLab Self-Managed**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, **Enter your GitLab domain name** or **Enter your GitLab group path** text field may appear.

![GitLab Self-Managed scanned by Vault Radar Agent Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/gitlab/gitlab-self-managed-agent-data-source.png)

1. If you previously selected **GitLab Self-Managed**, enter the domain URL in the **Enter your GitLab domain name** text field.
    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `gitlab.acme.com`

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources**, enter the top-level group path in the **Enter your GitLab group path** text field.

1. Create an environment variable for the API token in the agent environment.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export GITLAB_TOKEN=mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Enter the name of the environment variable in the **Enter environment path to your GitLab personal access token** text field with the `env://` prefix.

    **Example:**

    ```plaintext
    env://GITLAB_TOKEN
    ```

1. Click **Next**.

# Finish data source import

1. Choose which repositories to monitor by selecting either the **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**

    Monitor active repositories up to limit ![Add GitLab all active](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-all-active-resources.png)

    Monitor selected repositories ![Add GitLab custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-custom-select-resources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

# Finish data source host import

1. Choose which data sources to monitor by selecting either the **All data sources** or **Select data sources**

    Monitor all data sources up to limit ![Add GitLab all](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-all-active-data-sources.png)

    Monitor selected data sources ![Add GitLab custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-custom-select-data-sources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

# Update data source

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Sources**, and click the three dots to the right

   ![Update monitored resources](/img/docs/vault-radar/gitlab/gitlab-update-data-source.png)

1. To update the monitored repositories, click **Edit monitored resources**.

1. If you imported the **Data Source** directly, to update the token, click **Edit data source details**.

1. If these data sources were discovered by importing the **Data Source Host**, to update the token, [update the data source host instead](#update-data-source-host)

# Update data source host

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Source Hosts**, and click the three dots to the right

   ![Update monitored data sources](/img/docs/vault-radar/gitlab/gitlab-update-data-source-host.png)

1. To update the monitored data sources, click **Edit data sources**

1. To update the token, click **Edit data source host details**
